BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after the landlord mentioned property taxes might bump the showroom lease. Pre-review with Sarah Adams.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Overhead-only view — full-year net profit margin after inventory acquisition costs runs ~22% on ~$420,000 revenue. Need to revisit with Sarah Adams once Q4 actuals are closed.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $3,500/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed overhead costs $6,090 + $100 increase = $6,190. At an average sale/service margin of roughly $650, break-even is about 10 transactions/month.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within the current pace of 15-18 transactions per month.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case — rent to $3,900/mo (unlikely per landlord)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Landlord said this was unlikely but wanted to stress-test the showroom lease.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is roughly 9-10 sales/service transactions per month against fixed overhead. At the current pace of 15-18 transactions we have plenty of headroom, though winter (Dec-Jan) tends to run leaner before Modernism Week picks back up in February.
ActionItems: Wait for the landlord to confirm the property-tax pass-through before signing the lease renewal
Revisit after year-end with Sarah Adams for proper P&L modeling
Factor in the Shopify storefront launch's projected revenue once Tyler's build is live
